title: ulmus davidiana var. suberosa
intro: Ulmus davidiana var. suberosa, also known as the paperbark elm or Chinese paperbark elm, is a deciduous tree native to China. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Ulmus davidiana var. suberosa include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
